response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

***  

the approach to solve this problem is to iterate through each element in the array and calculate the required sum based on the given conditions. for each element at index i, if i is a multiple of 3, we calculate the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements. otherwise, we calculate the sum of absolute differences.  

to efficiently calculate the sum of absolute differences, we can use the prefix sum array. for a sorted array, the sum of absolute differences can be computed using the prefix sum array. for example, if the array is sorted, the sum of absolute differences for nums[i] can be calculated as i * nums[i] - prefix_sum[i], where prefix_sum[i] is the sum of the first i elements.  

for the square of the differences, we can use a similar approach, but instead of using the prefix sum, we can use the sum of squares of the elements. the sum of squares of the differences can be calculated as (i * nums[i]^2 - prefix_sum_sq[i]), where prefix_sum_sq[i] is the sum of squares of the first i elements.  

this approach ensures that we can compute the required sums efficiently in O(n) time complexity.  

the code for this problem would involve iterating through each element, checking if the index is a multiple of 3, and then calculating the appropriate sum based on the condition.
